scenario: Vanta Insurance models a Camunda 8.8 underwriting process that handles 5000 policies per day. A user task references a Camunda Form by formId on the zeebe:formDefinition element, but the modeler omits the bindingType attribute and must predict which binding the engine will apply when the user task activates for each running policy instance.
question: Which binding behavior applies when the zeebe:formDefinition omits the bindingType attribute on a Camunda user task?

options.text: The deployment binding applies and pins the form to the version deployed with the current process when bindingType is omitted on zeebe:formDefinition for the user task
The latest binding applies and resolves to the newest deployed version of the form at user task activation when bindingType is omitted on zeebe:formDefinition
The versionTag binding applies and resolves to the form matching the configured versionTag attribute when bindingType is omitted on zeebe:formDefinition
No form is resolved and the user task raises an incident at activation when bindingType is omitted on zeebe:formDefinition for the Camunda user task

a.text: Incorrect. The deployment binding only applies when bindingType is explicitly set to deployment; it is not the default value chosen when the attribute is omitted on zeebe:formDefinition.

b.text: Correct. The user tasks documentation states that if the bindingType attribute is not specified, latest is used as the default, so the engine resolves the form to its newest deployed version at user task activation.

c.text: Incorrect. The versionTag binding is only used when bindingType is explicitly set to versionTag and a versionTag attribute is supplied; it is never selected by default when bindingType is omitted.

d.text: Incorrect. Omitting bindingType is a supported configuration; the engine falls back to the documented latest default and does not raise an incident when the form is otherwise correctly linked.

explanation: On zeebe:formDefinition, bindingType is optional and defaults to latest when omitted, so the linked Camunda Form is resolved to its newest deployed version each time the user task activates.
